fact: October 12 is exactly 285 days into the year, which means there are only 80 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts right now - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single autumn!
The sun rises almost exactly due east and sets due west around this time, making it super easy to find directions without a compass.
Maple trees are pumping sugar down to their roots like crazy right now, which is why their leaves turn those brilliant reds and oranges.
October 12 falls smack in the middle of Libra season - the only zodiac sign represented by an object instead of a living thing!
Spiders are at their absolute biggest and fattest right now because they've been eating bugs all summer long.
The Northern Hemisphere is losing about 3 minutes of daylight every single day around October 12.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, spring is in full swing and baby animals are being born everywhere!
October 12 is roughly 3 weeks after the autumn equinox, which means nights are getting noticeably longer than days in the north.
Monarch butterflies are flying up to 100 miles per day right now on their epic journey to Mexico - some traveling over 2,000 miles total!
October 12 is prime time for seeing the most colorful sunsets of the year because of all the dust and particles floating in the crisp autumn air.
